---
title: "About"
image: profile.jpg
about:
  template: trestles
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/pibieta
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:ibieta.pablo@gmail.com
---

I write about the places where probability, physics, and inference meet —
mostly **information theory**, **causal inference**, **econometrics**, and
**machine learning**, with the occasional detour into the physics that
motivated me to learn this stuff in the first place.

## What you'll find here

Posts here tend to be one of three things:

- *Worked notes* — me working through a paper or technique carefully enough
  that I'd be willing to be quizzed on it.
- *Short essays* — opinionated takes on methodology, usually written
  after a debate I've had with myself or a colleague.
- *Simulations* — small computational experiments that test whether an
  intuition actually survives contact with data.

If anything here is wrong (and some of it surely is), please open an issue
on the [repo](https://github.com/pibieta/pibieta.github.io) — I'd rather
be corrected than confidently mistaken.
